data.title: Flykube ES & PT: Surprise European Getaways from €109
data.excerpt: Flykube ES & PT offers curated surprise trips across Europe starting at €109. Choose your dates & budget — they pick the destination. Book your escape now.

$media.alt: Flykube ES & PT: Surprise European Getaways from €109

children.text: *Disclosure: This post contains affiliate links. If you book through them, I may earn a small commission at no extra cost to you.*

Let me tell you about the last time I planned a weekend getaway. I spent three hours comparing flights, another two scrolling through hotels, and by the end of it I was so exhausted that I almost canceled the whole thing. Sound familiar?

Now imagine this: you pick your dates, set a budget, and someone else handles the rest — including picking the destination. You show up at the airport with a sealed envelope, and only then do you discover where you're going. That's exactly what Flykube ES & PT does, and it's one of the most refreshing approaches to travel I've come across.
Ready to skip the planning fatigue and let someone else surprise you?
Check Flykube's surprise trips here
What Is Flykube ES & PT?

Flykube is an online travel agency based in Spain and Portugal that specializes in mystery getaways across Europe. The concept is simple but brilliant: you choose your departure city, travel dates, trip duration, and budget range. Flykube's team then curates a complete travel package — flights, accommodation, and destination — that you only discover shortly before departure (or sometimes at the airport itself).

The service targets travelers in Spain and Portugal primarily, with departures from major cities like Madrid, Barcelona, Lisbon, and Porto. Destinations span across Europe's most beloved cities and coastal spots: think Lisbon, Rome, Amsterdam, Santorini, the Canary Islands, and dozens more.

Prices start at just €109 per person for short getaways, which includes round-trip flights and hotel accommodation. That's a weekend in a European capital for less than what some people spend on dinner and drinks in their own city.

The brand has built a solid reputation too. Flykube holds a 4.3-star rating on Trustpilot from over 2,000 traveler reviews — a strong signal that the concept delivers real value and memorable experiences.
How Flykube Works: Step by Step
The booking process is designed to be as frictionless as the trip itself. Here's how it breaks down:
Step 1: Choose Your Departure City
Flykube currently operates departures from several airports in Spain and Portugal. You select the one closest to you — Madrid, Barcelona, Valencia, Seville, Bilbao, Lisbon, Porto, and others.
Step 2: Pick Your Dates
You choose a weekend, a week, or whatever window works for your schedule. Flykube shows availability across their calendar so you can find slots that match your life.
Step 3: Set Your Budget

This is where it gets interesting. Flykube offers multiple budget tiers, starting from €109 and scaling up depending on trip length and comfort level. Each tier guarantees flights and accommodation, with higher tiers offering better hotels or longer stays.
Step 4: Add Preferences (Optional)

You can indicate preferences like "beach destination" or "city break," whether you want a warmer climate, or if you've already visited certain cities and want to avoid repeats. Flykube uses this to tailor your surprise.
Step 5: Receive Your Surprise

After booking, you'll get clues or a sealed reveal (depending on the package type). Some travelers receive their destination a few days before departure; others find out at the airport. Either way, the anticipation is part of the magic.
Step 6: Travel
Flights and hotel are sorted. You just show up, explore, and enjoy a destination you might never have chosen yourself — but that Flykube's team handpicked for you.
If you want a detailed walkthrough, we've put together a
complete step-by-step guide to using Flykube
New & Featured Functions
Flykube has been iterating on their model, and several features stand out as particularly worth knowing about:
Summer Surprise Packages

Flykube's seasonal summer deals are some of their most popular offerings. Beach vacations starting from €199 give travelers access to Mediterranean and Atlantic coastal destinations during peak season — a time when independent booking usually costs significantly more.
Flexible Date Ranges

Rather than locking you into exact dates, Flykube now lets you specify a range. This gives their team more options to find the best flight deals, which often translates into better destinations or upgraded accommodations within your budget.
Couple and Group Packages

Originally focused on solo and couple travel, Flykube has expanded into group packages. You can book for two, three, or four travelers, making it a fantastic option for friend trips where nobody wants to take on the role of "trip planner."
Destination Clues Before Departure

Some packages now include playful clues — a weather forecast screenshot, a hint about local cuisine, or a blurred photo of a landmark. It turns the lead-up to your trip into a game, and honestly, it's addictive.
Repeat Destination Avoidance
If you've used Flykube before, you can flag cities you've already visited. Their algorithm (and human curation team) works to ensure you get a fresh experience every time.
We keep a running list of
Flykube's newest features and updates
if you want to stay in the loop.
Who Is Flykube Ideal For?
This service isn't for every type of traveler — and that's fine. But for certain people, it's a perfect match:
Decision-fatigued professionals

If you work a demanding job and the last thing you want to do on your weekend is research hotels and compare flight prices, Flykube eliminates that entire burden. You make three decisions (dates, budget, departure city) and the rest is handled.
Couples looking for spontaneous romance

There's something inherently romantic about a surprise trip. You and your partner showing up at the airport, opening an envelope, and discovering you're flying to Lisbon or Dubrovnik? That's a story you'll tell for years.
First-time European travelers

If you're based in Spain or Portugal and want to explore more of the continent but feel overwhelmed by options, Flykube curates the experience for you. Every destination in their pool is vetted and traveler-approved.
Budget-conscious adventurers
Starting at €109 for a full package (flights + hotel), Flykube often beats what you'd spend booking independently. Their bulk purchasing power with airlines and hotels means they pass savings to you.
Groups who can't agree on a destination

We've all been in that WhatsApp group where someone suggests Barcelona, another person counters with Prague, and the conversation goes nowhere for two weeks. Flykube sidesteps the debate entirely. The destination is chosen for you. Problem solved.
Gift givers
Looking for a birthday or anniversary gift that isn't another gift card? A Flykube surprise trip is genuinely one of the most exciting presents you can give someone.
Explore Flykube's gift-friendly packages here
Want more ideas on how different types of travelers use the platform? Our
Flykube use cases guide
breaks it down.
Why Flykube Stands Out in the Travel Space
Plenty of travel agencies exist. Budget airlines run their own flash sales. Booking platforms offer "mystery hotel" deals. So what makes Flykube different?
Human curation, not just algorithms

Flykube doesn't just pair the cheapest flight with a random hotel. Their team reviews destinations, checks accommodation quality, and considers factors like seasonality, local events, and traveler preferences. The result feels curated, not randomized.
The surprise element changes how you travel

There's actual psychology behind why surprise trips feel more rewarding. When you don't spend weeks anticipating a specific destination, you arrive with fewer expectations and more openness. Travelers consistently report that Flykube trips feel more adventurous and memorable than equivalent self-planned vacations.
Transparent pricing with no hidden fees

The price you see is the price you pay. Flights and accommodation are included. There's no moment at checkout where resort fees or baggage charges suddenly inflate the total. What you budget is what you spend (minus personal expenses at the destination, obviously).
Spain and Portugal departure focus

Rather than trying to serve every market, Flykube has deepened their expertise in Iberian departures. This means better airline relationships, more route options, and a destination pool specifically curated for travelers based in these regions.
Strong community and review base

With 2,000+ Trustpilot reviews and a 4.3-star average, Flykube has a proven track record. The reviews aren't just positive — they're enthusiastic. Travelers share photos, recount surprise reveals, and often mention booking again.
If you're weighing your options, our breakdown of
why Flykube is worth choosing
dives deeper into the comparison landscape.
Real Traveler Experiences
The proof of any travel service is in the stories people tell after. Here are a few patterns that show up repeatedly in Flykube reviews:
"I never would have gone there on my own"

This is the single most common sentiment. Travelers discover cities like Porto, Tallinn, Split, or Seville — places they hadn't considered — and come back raving. Flykube pushes you out of your comfort zone in the best way.
"The hotel was better than expected"

A recurring surprise. Because Flykube negotiates directly with accommodation providers, they often secure properties at a quality level above what the price point suggests. Reviews frequently mention central locations, clean rooms, and friendly staff.
"We've booked three times now"
Repeat bookings are a strong signal. When travelers come back not once but twice, it means the first experience delivered. Flykube's repeat customer rate appears robust based on review patterns.
"The reveal was the highlight"

Whether it's opening an envelope at the airport or decoding clues in the days before departure, the surprise element consistently ranks as the most exciting part of the experience. It transforms a simple vacation into an event.
For practical advice from seasoned Flykube travelers, check out our
tips and best practices guide
How Flykube Compares to Planning Your Own Trips

Let's be honest: some people love the planning process. If you enjoy spending Sunday evenings on Skyscanner, cross-referencing Google Maps with TripAdvisor reviews, and building spreadsheets for your weekend trips — Flykube might not replace that hobby for you.
But for everyone else? The math is compelling:
Time saved
: 5+ hours per trip that you'd otherwise spend researching, comparing, and booking.
Money saved
: Flykube's bulk deals frequently undercut independent booking by 15-30%.
Decision fatigue eliminated
: Three inputs (dates, budget, departure city) instead of dozens of micro-decisions.
Discovery factor
: You visit places you'd never pick yourself, which often turn out to be your favorite trips.
Flykube doesn't compete with DIY travel planning. It competes with the paralysis of not traveling at all because planning feels like too much work. And it wins that contest every time.
Pricing and What's Included
Here's a general breakdown of Flykube's pricing tiers (exact prices fluctuate by season and departure city):
| Package | Duration | Starting Price | Includes |
| Weekend Getaway | 2-3 nights | €109-€149 | Flights + Hotel |
| Short Break | 3-4 nights | €149-€199 | Flights + Hotel |
| Summer Surprise | 3-5 nights | €199-€299 | Flights + Hotel (beach destinations) |
| Extended Escape | 5-7 nights | €249-€399 | Flights + Hotel |

All packages include round-trip flights from your selected departure city and accommodation for the full duration. Baggage policies depend on the airline selected, and Flykube provides clear information about this during booking.
The value proposition is straightforward: for the price of a nice dinner for two in most European capitals, you get an entire weekend away in a new city.
See Flykube's current deals and availability
Getting Started with Flykube
If you've read this far, I'll assume the concept has your attention. Here's how to take action:
1. Visit
flykube.com
and select your departure region (Spain or Portugal).
2. Browse available date windows for your preferred city.
3. Choose a budget tier that fits your comfort level.
4. Add any preferences (beach, city, climate, places to avoid).
5. Complete booking and wait for the surprise.
That's it. Five steps, and you'll have a European getaway on your calendar that you didn't have to agonize over.

Flykube represents a shift in how we think about travel planning. Not everything needs to be optimized, spreadsheeted, and over-researched. Sometimes the best trips are the ones where you hand the reins to someone else and let the surprise carry you somewhere new.

children.text: What is Flykube and how does it work?

Flykube is an online travel agency specializing in surprise European getaways. You choose your departure city, travel dates, and budget. Flykube then selects the destination, books your flights and hotel, and reveals where you're going either before departure or at the airport. Prices start at €109 per person including flights and accommodation.
Is Flykube legit? What do reviews say?

Yes, Flykube is a legitimate travel agency with a 4.3-star rating on Trustpilot from over 2,000 traveler reviews. Customers consistently praise the value for money, the quality of accommodations, and the excitement of the surprise element. Repeat bookings are common, which speaks to consistent customer satisfaction.
Can I choose a specific type of destination with Flykube?

While you can't pick the exact city, Flykube allows you to set preferences such as beach destinations, city breaks, warmer climates, or specific regions to avoid. You can also flag cities you've already visited so Flykube's team ensures a fresh experience. The surprise is curated, not random.
What's included in a Flykube package?

Every Flykube package includes round-trip flights from your selected departure city and hotel accommodation for the full trip duration. Pricing is transparent with no hidden fees. Baggage policies vary by airline and are communicated during booking. Personal expenses at the destination (food, activities, transport) are not included.
